commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=a4c2210c6eeae719278c44a65b3347498b75c339)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Control UI validation and tests for Telegram cron announce To values, adds a Telegram-specific i18n error string across locale bundles, updates raw-copy metadata, and changes two cron view labels to use i18n.

PR surface: Source +150, Tests +48. Total +198 across 23 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and v2026.6.6 validate webhook deliveryTo only, while the linked issue's value flows to Telegram delivery and the PR demonstrates the missing save-time check with controller tests.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real dashboard proof, and maintainers need a repaired branch or replacement that preserves the Telegram target contract before merge.

target.title: fix(cron): validate Telegram delivery target is numeric chat ID
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- The cron dashboard accepted arbitrary text for Telegram announce delivery "To" field, causing runtime failures with "Telegram recipient must be a numeric chat ID".
- Validation now checks if the target is a numeric chat ID when the delivery channel is Telegram.

Why does this matter now?

- Users can enter non-numeric text (e.g. "my group", "@username") in the Telegram delivery target field.
- The job saves successfully but fails at runtime with a confusing error message.

What is the intended outcome?

- Non-numeric Telegram delivery targets show a validation error before saving.
- Valid numeric chat IDs (including negative IDs like -1001234567890) are accepted.
- Empty targets are accepted (the job will use the default delivery target).

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to other delivery channels or cron job execution.
- No changes to the Telegram bot message sending logic.

## Linked context

Closes #90467

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Cron dashboard accepts non-numeric text for Telegram delivery target, causing runtime failures.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node.js v22.11.0, OpenClaw latest main (e99ab385cf).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Read ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.ts and confirmed the validation logic rejects non-numeric Telegram targets (lines 171-176).
  - Ran regression test: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts
- Evidence after fix: Regression test output:
  Tests 3 passed (3)
  rejects non-numeric Telegram delivery target (regression #90467)
  accepts numeric Telegram delivery target
  accepts empty Telegram delivery target
- Observed result after fix: Non-numeric Telegram delivery targets show "cron.errors.telegramChatIdRequired" validation error; numeric targets and empty targets pass validation.
- What was not tested: Live cron job creation with Telegram delivery (requires Telegram bot setup).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The regression test uses the form validation function directly; it does not prove the Telegram bot delivery behavior.

## Tests and validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts — 3 new tests passed

## Risk checklist

- userVisible: Yes (cron dashboard now validates Telegram delivery targets)
- configEnvMigration: No
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Only adds validation; no behavior change for valid numeric chat IDs.

## Current review state

- Pre-existing CI failures (not from this PR): checks-node-core-runtime-media-ui is a flaky test unrelated to this change.
- Ready for review after this update is pushed.
- [x] AI-assisted (built with Claude Code)
